reading_prompt: What did the Emancipation Proclamation accomplish during the war?
writing_sentence: The Emancipation Proclamation freed slaves in the Southern states.

reading_prompt: What is the capital city of your state?
writing_sentence: The capital city varies depending on where you reside.

reading_prompt: What are the two parts of the United States Congress?
writing_sentence: The Senate and House of Representatives make up Congress.

reading_prompt: Who is the Commander in Chief of the United States military?
writing_sentence: The President is the Commander in Chief of the military.

reading_prompt: What is the head executive of a state government called?
writing_sentence: The head executive of a state government is the Governor.

reading_prompt: What is the supreme judicial body in the United States?
writing_sentence: The Supreme Court is the highest court in the United States.

reading_prompt: What stops one branch of government from becoming too powerful?
writing_sentence: Checks and balances stop one branch from becoming too powerful.

reading_prompt: Who is in charge of the executive branch of government?
writing_sentence: The President is in charge of the executive branch.

reading_prompt: What is one promise you make when you become a United States citizen?
writing_sentence: I promise to be loyal to the United States of America.

reading_prompt: How old do citizens have to be to vote for President?
writing_sentence: Citizens must be 18 and older to vote for President.

reading_prompt: What are two ways that Americans can participate in their democracy?
writing_sentence: Americans can participate by voting and joining a political party.

reading_prompt: When is the last day you can send in federal income tax forms?
writing_sentence: The last day to file federal income taxes is April 15th.

reading_prompt: Who was the president during World War One?
writing_sentence: Woodrow Wilson was the president during World War One.

reading_prompt: Who was the president during the Great Depression and World War Two?
writing_sentence: Franklin D. Roosevelt was president during World War Two.

reading_prompt: Who did the United States fight against in World War Two?
writing_sentence: The United States fought Japan, Germany, and Italy in World War Two.

reading_prompt: Before he was president, Eisenhower was a general in which war?
writing_sentence: Eisenhower was a famous general in World War Two.

reading_prompt: During the Cold War, what was the main concern of the United States?
writing_sentence: The spread of communism was the main concern during the Cold War.

reading_prompt: What movement tried to end racial discrimination in the United States?
writing_sentence: The civil rights movement tried to end racial discrimination.

reading_prompt: What major event happened on September 11, 2001, in the United States?
writing_sentence: Terrorists attacked the United States on September 11, 2001.

reading_prompt: Name one American Indian tribe indigenous to the United States.
writing_sentence: The Cherokee are an American Indian tribe in the United States.

reading_prompt: What are the two longest rivers located in the United States?
writing_sentence: The Mississippi and Missouri rivers are the longest in the country.

reading_prompt: What is the name of the United States national anthem?
writing_sentence: The Star-Spangled Banner is the United States national anthem.

reading_prompt: Why does the American flag have 50 stars on it?
writing_sentence: The flag has 50 stars because there is one star for each state.

reading_prompt: Why does the American flag have 13 stripes on it?
writing_sentence: The flag has 13 stripes to represent the original 13 colonies.

reading_prompt: What is the name of the statue located in New York Harbor?
writing_sentence: The Statue of Liberty is located in New York Harbor.
